questionSummary: Copy-ready maintainer/source clarification question for supported noninteractive OpenClaw plugin-tool discovery and invocation, specifically for verifying the read-only Envoy tool.

environmentDetails.blocker: No supported documented noninteractive way was proven to list or invoke loaded plugin tools from the installed OpenClaw CLI/runtime surface.

maintainerQuestions: What is the supported noninteractive command or RPC method to list loaded plugin tools?
What is the supported noninteractive command or RPC method to invoke a loaded plugin tool?
Does openclaw gateway call support plugin tool invocation? If yes, what method name and payload shape should be used?
Is there a JSON-RPC method equivalent to plugin.tools.list, plugin.tools.inspect, or plugin.tools.call?
Where in source are gateway RPC methods registered?
Where in source are plugin manifests loaded into runtime state?
Where in source are plugin tools exposed to the model/UI?
Are plugin tools callable outside the model loop?
Is there a read-only tool metadata flag or safety classification already supported?
What is the intended way to verify a plugin tool is callable after local install?
desiredResponseFields: whether the path is public/supported or internal/unsupported
